answer context: Anyone can file or submit a FOIA request to a federal agency. You do not have to be a lawyer, a U.S. citizen, or of a certain age. You can submit a request as an individual, or on behalf of your organization or group. In many cases submitting on behalf of a group can be helpful, because you can show that your group is committed to addressing issues of public interest and that the documents you get through FOIA will be distributed widely. These factors may help you obtain a fee waiver and avoid the government charging you for the costs of processing your request.
